images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of the EasyVideoR1 training pipeline. Videos are preprocessed offline into.ptcache files. During training, each worker loads cached frames locally.

Figure 2:Benchmark performance comparison. The number above each blue bar indicates the accuracy change relative to the Instruct baseline. Background colors denote benchmark categories. EasyVideoR1 training yields an average improvement of +2.3 points, with the largest gains on reasoning (+6.6 on Video-Holmes) and mathematical (+6.7 on VideoMathQA) tasks.

Figure 3:Training efficiency comparison between cache-based loading and on-the-fly video decoding. (a) Per-step time breakdown by pipeline phase. Cache-based loading reduces rollout generation time by 1.5×\timesand reference model forward time by 2.9×\times, while actor update time remains unchanged. (b) Overall efficiency: cache-based loading achieves a 1.47×\timesspeedup in both wall-clock time per step and token throughput.
